metadata.name: Federal Employees' Group Life Insurance (FEGLI)
metadata.description: FEGLI coverage structure, premium rates by age band, retirement reduction elections, Extra Benefit schedule, and coverage calculation rules. Current rates effective October 1, 2021 per OPM Federal Register notice (86 FR 50382).
sources: Federal Register 86 FR 50382 (September 8, 2021) — FEGLI Premium Rate Changes
notes: FEGLI is the largest group life insurance program in the world — over 4 million enrollees.
Administered by OPM, underwritten by Metropolitan Life Insurance Company (MetLife).
FEGLI is term life insurance — no cash value, no loan value, no paid-up value.
Rates shown are current as of October 2021. OPM periodically adjusts rates based on actuarial experience.
Rate changes require a Federal Register notice — not annual like FEHB.

rate_effective_note: First pay period on or after October 1, 2021. These are the most recent rates as of March 2026.

basic.description: Automatic coverage for eligible employees unless waived
basic.formula: Annual basic pay rounded up to next $1,000, plus $2,000
basic.minimum_bia_note: Basic coverage is at least $10,000 regardless of salary
basic.accidental_death: Basic includes Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) benefit equal to the BIA, payable in addition to the life insurance

option_a_standard.description: Additional $10,000 of coverage on the employee
option_a_standard.enrollment: Must be elected — not automatic. Requires Basic coverage.

option_b_additional.description: Additional coverage in multiples (1–5) of annual basic pay
option_b_additional.multiple_basis: Annual basic pay rounded up to next $1,000 (no $2,000 add-on)
option_b_additional.enrollment: Must be elected — not automatic. Requires Basic coverage.

option_c_family.description: Coverage on spouse and eligible dependent children
option_c_family.eligible_children: Unmarried dependent children under age 22 (including stepchildren and adopted children)
option_c_family.enrollment: Must be elected — not automatic. Requires Basic coverage.

extra_benefit_under_45.description: Free additional Basic coverage for employees under age 45
extra_benefit_under_45.note: The Extra Benefit doubles Basic coverage at age 35 or under and decreases 10% per year until age 45. No additional premium is charged.

employee_premium_rates._comment: Rates effective first pay period on or after October 1, 2021

basic.note: Same rate for all ages. Government pays additional ~$0.08/biweekly per $1,000 (one-third share).

option_a.note: Total premium for the flat $10,000 Option A coverage. Not per-$1,000.

option_c.note: Rate is based on the EMPLOYEE's age, not the spouse's or child's age

retirement_reduction_elections.description: At retirement (or age 65, whichever is later), retirees must choose a reduction schedule for Basic, Option A, Option B, and Option C
retirement_reduction_elections.election_timing: Must be made at retirement — cannot be changed later except to reduce coverage further

basic_options.description: Coverage reduces 2% per month after age 65 until reaching 25% of pre-retirement BIA
basic_options.note: This is the free option and the default if no election is made

basic_options.description: Coverage reduces 1% per month after age 65 until reaching 50% of pre-retirement BIA
basic_options.premium_note: Premiums continue for life (unless cancelled or switched to 75% Reduction)

basic_options.description: Coverage remains at 100% of pre-retirement BIA for life
basic_options.premium_note: Premiums continue for life (unless cancelled or switched to a reduction option)

option_a_retirement.with_75pct_reduction: Reduces 2% per month after 65 to 25% of $10,000 ($2,500). Free after reaching minimum.
option_a_retirement.with_no_reduction: Remains at full $10,000. Free after age 65.
option_a_retirement.note: Option A only has 75% Reduction or No Reduction choices

option_b_retirement.with_75pct_reduction: Reduces 2% per month after 65 to 25% of elected multiple, then free

with_no_reduction.description: Retains full coverage at elected multiples. Premiums continue at post-retirement rates.
with_no_reduction.cost_warning: Option B No Reduction becomes extremely expensive. For a retiree with $100,000 salary (5× = $500,000 coverage), monthly cost at age 80+ is $3,120.

option_c_retirement.with_75pct_reduction: Reduces 2% per month after 65 to 25% of elected multiples, then free

with_no_reduction.description: Retains full coverage. Premiums continue at post-retirement rates.

eligibility_to_continue_in_retirement.requirement: Must have been continuously enrolled in FEGLI for the 5 years immediately preceding retirement, OR from the earliest date coverage was available (if employed fewer than 5 years)
eligibility_to_continue_in_retirement.note: If you waived or cancelled coverage during the 5 years before retirement, you may not be able to continue coverage into retirement

assignment_of_benefits.description: FEGLI coverage can be irrevocably assigned to another person, trust, or entity
assignment_of_benefits.effect: Assignee becomes the owner of the policy — controls beneficiary designation and cannot be changed by the insured
assignment_of_benefits.form: SF 2823 — Assignment of Federal Employees' Group Life Insurance
assignment_of_benefits.note: Assignment is irrevocable. Once assigned, the insured cannot cancel or change the coverage.

basic_premium.taxable_income: The government's share of the Basic premium (one-third) is tax-free for coverage up to $50,000. Coverage over $50,000 has imputed income for the government share.
basic_premium.over_50000_note: IRS Table I applies to determine the taxable value of coverage over $50,000. This is reported as imputed income on the W-2.

death_benefit.federal_tax: FEGLI death benefits are generally tax-free to beneficiaries under IRC § 101(a)
death_benefit.estate_tax: May be included in the insured's gross estate if the insured retained incidents of ownership (unless assigned)

living_benefits.description: Terminally ill employees or retirees may elect a Living Benefit — an accelerated payment of Basic coverage
living_benefits.eligibility: Medical prognosis of 9 months or less to live
living_benefits.amount: Up to the full Basic Insurance Amount
living_benefits.effect: Reduces the death benefit by the amount of the Living Benefit
